text: and stem explants were cultured on media containing seven combinations of auxins and cytokinins, and evaluated for callus induction frequency, biomass accumulation, and alkaloid content after eight weeks. Stem explants exhibited superior responsiveness, achieving 100% callus induction under most PGR treatments, whereas the combination of GA₃ and BAP failed to induce callogenesis in both explant types. HPLC analysis revealed that the combination of 1.5 mg/L 2,4-D and 1.5 mg/L kinetin (Kin) in leaf-derived callus yielded the highest CAT and VBL concentrations (5.01 µg/gFW and 1.96 µg/gFW; respectively).
